lines.en_lit: What, is not about those who won the lottery, Rav Huna bar Yehuda said Rav Sheshet said, No, about those who did not win the lottery.

So too, it is reasonable As, if it were to enter your mind with those who won the lottery They would leave only their trousers on them? But wasn’t it taught From where that nothing should precede the trousers The verse states: “And he shall have linen trousers upon his flesh”

And the other one, this is not difficult, this is what is teaching: While the non-sacred garments are still on them they put the sacred trousers on them, and they remove the non-sacred clothes, and they left only the trousers.

Rav Sheshet said: From where do I say As it was taught The Chamber of Hewn Stone was in the style of a large basilica [basileki]; the lottery in the east and an Elder sits in its west And the priests stand in a circle in the shape of a bracelet [bekholyar], and the appointed comes and removes the mitre from the head of one of them, and knew that the count began from him. And if it were to enter your mind non-sacred garments is there a mitre among non-sacred garments?
Yes, as Rav Yehuda, and some say Rav Shmuel bar Yehuda, taught: A priest whose mother made a tunic for him, may perform an individual service with it on,

Abaye said: Conclude from this the Chamber of Hewn Stone half in the sacred and half in the non-sacred And conclude from it had two doorways, one opened to the sacred and one opened to the non-sacred As, were it to enter your mind entirely in the sacred an Elder sat in its west? Didn’t the Master say: There is no sitting in the courtyard except for kings of the house of David alone?

And if it were to enter your mind entirely in the non-sacred the lottery in the east? Aren’t we required “In the House of God we walked with the throng” there not be Rather, conclude from this half in the sacred and half in the non-sacred

And were it to enter your mind had one doorway, which opened to the sacred Elder sit in its west? Didn’t we learn Chambers that are built in the non-sacred open up into the sacred their interior is sacred, And if, it were to enter your mind which opened to the non-sacred lottery in its east? Didn’t we learn built in the sacred open up into the non-sacred within them is non-sacred, Rather, isn’t it conclude from this had two doorways, one that opened up into the sacred and one that opened into the non-sacred
MISHNA: The second lottery Who slaughters who sprinkles who removes the ashes the inner altar, and who removes the ashes the candelabrum, and who takes the limbs up to the ramp

The head and the leg and the two forelegs The tail, and the leg And the breast and the throat And the two flanks and the intestines And the fine flour And the griddle-cake offering and the wine thirteen priests prevailed in this
Ben Azzai said before Rabbi Akiva in the name of Rabbi Yehoshua: it was sacrificed the way it walks

GEMARA: A dilemma was raised before When the priests performed the lottery, did they perform a lottery for one service, or perhaps they performed a lottery for each and every service Come hear There were four lotteries there. And if it were to enter your mind they performed a lottery each and every service there would be many Rav Naḥman bar Yitzḥak said: this is what is saying: gathered four times for a lottery, but each many lotteries
